title: WinForms event handler without try/catch — PipelineStoppedException crash
description: WinForms event handlers (Add_Click, Add_Tick, Add_FormClosing, Add_SelectedIndexChanged, etc.) that lack a try/catch wrapper will propagate any unhandled error as System.Management.Automation.PipelineStoppedException. PowerShell cannot display the inner error because the WinForms message loop swallows context. This causes silent crashes or cryptic 'The pipeline has been stopped' dialogs with no useful stack trace. Timer Tick handlers are especially dangerous because they fire repeatedly on the UI thread. Combined with .GetNewClosure() $script: scope issues (P024) and null-reference risks (P022), unguarded handlers are the #1 source of PipelineStoppedException in this project.
detection_method: Regex scan for .Add_<EventName>({ without try/catch as first statement in block
preventionRule: Every WinForms event handler script block MUST wrap its body in try/catch. At minimum: catch { Write-AppLog "Handler error: $_" -Level Error }. For timer Tick handlers, also call $this.Stop() in the catch to prevent repeated crashes.
remedy:
Wrap the handler body:
  BEFORE: $btn.Add_Click({ $form.Close() })
  AFTER:  $btn.Add_Click({ try { $form.Close() } catch { Write-AppLog "Click error: $_" -Level Error } })

For timer Tick handlers:
  $timer.Add_Tick({ try { <body> } catch { $this.Stop(); Write-AppLog "Tick error: $_" -Level Error } })

known_instances.risk: CRITICAL — fires every 500ms, accesses $shared.RunningJobs dictionary keys that may not exist, Receive-Job can fail
known_instances.notes: Also uses .GetNewClosure() with $shared hashtable — container pattern is correct but body needs try/catch

known_instances.risk: CRITICAL — same pattern as line 352 for Run All button
known_instances.notes: Duplicate timer tick handler for batch scan execution

known_instances.risk: HIGH — accesses $script:_rlpRemaining via .GetNewClosure(), ShowDialog() at line 470 without Set-StrictMode -Off guard
known_instances.notes: Compound risk: P024 (StrictMode bleed) + P029 (unprotected handler) + .GetNewClosure() $script: issue

known_instances.risk: MEDIUM — Form.Close() or DialogResult assignment can throw during disposal
MEDIUM — Form.Close() can throw if disposal race occurs
HIGH — nested ShowDialog() call, array concatenation, logging — any failure propagates
HIGH — .SelectedItems[0] access, nested ShowDialog(), array rewrite
known_instances.notes: Has .Count check but no error boundary for the dialog call
Has .Count check but no catch for downstream failures
